examples.title: FCFS Disk Scheduling
examples.description: Implementation of First Come First Served disk scheduling algorithm
examples.code:
def fcfs_disk_scheduling(requests, head):
    """
    FCFS Disk Scheduling
    Args: requests - list of track numbers to service
          head - initial position of disk head
    Returns: total head movement
    """
    total_seek = 0
    current_head = head
    
    print(f"Initial head position: {head}")
    print("\nFCFS Service Order:")
    
    for i, request in enumerate(requests):
        seek_distance = abs(current_head - request)
        total_seek += seek_distance
        print(f"Step {i+1}: Head moves from {current_head} to {request}, seek distance: {seek_distance}")
        current_head = request
    
    print(f"\nTotal seek time: {total_seek}")
    return total_seek

# Example usage
requests = [95, 180, 34, 119, 12, 190]
head_start = 50
fcfs_disk_scheduling(requests, head_start)
examples.explanation: This FCFS implementation services requests in exact order of arrival, calculating seek distance for each movement and summing them for total seek time.

examples.title: SSTF Disk Scheduling
examples.description: Implementation of Shortest Seek Time First algorithm
examples.code:
def sstf_disk_scheduling(requests, head):
    """
    SSTF Disk Scheduling
    Services the request closest to current head position
    """
    total_seek = 0
    current_head = head
    remaining = requests.copy()  # List of pending requests
    service_order = []
    
    print(f"Initial head position: {head}")
    print("\nSSTF Service Order:")
    
    while remaining:
        # Find request with minimum seek time
        min_distance = float('inf')
        nearest_idx = 0
        
        for i, request in enumerate(remaining):
            distance = abs(current_head - request)
            if distance < min_distance:
                min_distance = distance
                nearest_idx = i
        
        # Service the nearest request
        request = remaining.pop(nearest_idx)
        total_seek += min_distance
        service_order.append(request)
        print(f"Head moves from {current_head} to {request}, seek distance: {min_distance}")
        current_head = request
    
    print(f"\nService order: {service_order}")
    print(f"Total seek time: {total_seek}")
    return total_seek

# Example usage
requests = [95, 180, 34, 119, 12, 190]
head_start = 50
sstf_disk_scheduling(requests, head_start)
examples.explanation: SSTF always selects the request closest to the current head position, minimizing each individual seek. Uses linear search to find nearest request each iteration.

examples.title: SCAN Disk Scheduling (Elevator Algorithm)
examples.description: Implementation of SCAN algorithm that moves to one end then reverses
examples.code:
def scan_disk_scheduling(requests, head, disk_size=200):
    """
    SCAN Disk Scheduling (Elevator Algorithm)
    Moves head to one end, servicing requests, then reverses
    """
    # Sort requests
    sorted_requests = sorted(requests)
    total_seek = 0
    current_head = head
    service_order = []
    
    print(f"Initial head position: {head}")
    print(f"Disk size: {disk_size}")
    print("\nSCAN Service Order:")
    
    # Find split point (first request > head)
    split_idx = 0
    for i, req in enumerate(sorted_requests):
        if req >= head:
            split_idx = i
            break
    
    # Service requests greater than head (moving right)
    for request in sorted_requests[split_idx:]:
        seek_distance = abs(current_head - request)
        total_seek += seek_distance
        service_order.append(request)
        print(f"Move {current_head} → {request}, distance: {seek_distance}")
        current_head = request
    
    # Go to end of disk
    if current_head < disk_size - 1:
        seek_distance = abs(current_head - (disk_size - 1))
        total_seek += seek_distance
        print(f"Move to end: {current_head} → {disk_size-1}, distance: {seek_distance}")
        current_head = disk_size - 1
    
    # Reverse and service remaining requests (moving left)
    for request in reversed(sorted_requests[:split_idx]):
        seek_distance = abs(current_head - request)
        total_seek += seek_distance
        service_order.append(request)
        print(f"Move {current_head} → {request}, distance: {seek_distance}")
        current_head = request
    
    print(f"\nService order: {service_order}")
    print(f"Total seek time: {total_seek}")
    return total_seek

# Example usage
requests = [95, 180, 34, 119, 12, 190]
head_start = 50
scan_disk_scheduling(requests, head_start)
examples.explanation: SCAN sorts requests and services all requests in one direction, then goes to the end and reverses to service remaining requests. This ensures no starvation.

examples.title: C-SCAN Disk Scheduling in C
examples.description: Implementation of C-SCAN algorithm providing uniform wait times
examples.explanation: C-SCAN treats the disk as circular, servicing requests in one direction then jumping back to the beginning without servicing requests on the return path, providing uniform wait times.
